sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, value DOUBLE PRECISION, ip INET);
INSERT INTO test_table (value, ip) VALUES (2.0, inet '192.168.1.5'), (NULL, inet '192.168.1.4'), (1.0, inet '192.168.1.6');
SELECT ln(value) AS logarithm, COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL GROUP BY ln(value);
SELECT * FROM test_table WHERE ln(value) <> ln(2.0) AND ip <> inet '192.168.1.4';
SELECT id, ln(value) AS logarithm, ip FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL AND ln(value) >= 0;
SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM test_table WHERE ln(value) <> ln(1.0) AND ip <> inet '192.168.1.5');
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL) > 0 AND ln(value) IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE temp_table AS SELECT id, ln(value) AS logarithm FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL HAVING logarithm <> 0;
SELECT DISTINCT value FROM test_table WHERE NOT (ln(value) IS NULL) AND ip <> inet '192.168.1.6' ORDER BY ln(value);
UPDATE test_table SET value = 0 WHERE ln(value) <> ln(0) AND value IS NOT NULL;
